evals.prompt: Estoy empezando un proyecto Next.js para un cliente (restaurante). ¿Cómo configuro el entorno en Vercel para separar staging y producción? Quiero que las PRs tengan preview automático, staging en una rama separada y producción en main.
evals.expected_output: Explanation of branch→environment mapping (main→prod, staging→staging, feature/*→preview), vercel.json configuration with regions and headers, env vars strategy per environment, and vercel CLI setup commands.

assertions.description: Mentions all three environments: production (main), staging, and preview (feature branches/PRs)
Includes a vercel.json configuration snippet with at least buildCommand, framework, or regions
Explicitly explains which branch maps to which environment (main→production, staging→staging)
Recommends cdg1 (Paris) or an EU region for a Spanish/EU restaurant client
Mentions 'vercel env pull' or how to sync environment variables locally

evals.prompt: Mi app Next.js tiene muchas variables de entorno: la API key de Resend, la URL del sitio, un secreto para revalidación ISR, y la URL de la base de datos. ¿Cómo las manejo correctamente entre desarrollo, staging y producción? ¿Qué va en .env.local, qué en Vercel, qué en .gitignore?
evals.expected_output: Clear distinction between NEXT_PUBLIC_* (client-safe) and private server-only vars, .env.example committed with placeholder values, .env.local in .gitignore, Vercel dashboard env vars per environment, and a startup validation pattern (lib/env.ts).

assertions.description: Distinguishes NEXT_PUBLIC_* (exposed to browser) from private server-only variables
Mentions .env.local should be in .gitignore and never committed
Recommends committing a .env.example with placeholder values (no real secrets)
Includes code for validating required env vars at startup (fail-fast pattern, lib/env.ts or similar)
Explains how to set different values per environment in Vercel (Production vs Preview vs Development)
